change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API

en.value: Based on the value chain assessment, CRS COVID-19 agribusiness impact assessment study recommendations and GoN (including local municipalities) priorities, CRS Nepal will design and implement an inclusive value chain project in Palpa District. The project will not only address the short-term economic recovery needs of vulnerable households but also make their livelihood more profitable and resilient to future shocks through their integration with value chains. Based on inputs from local municipalities and feasibility assessment, the project will provide support to agribusiness SMEs to recover from the impacts of COVID-19. The support will include improved processing equipment and infrastructure (warehouse, crop storage models, drying yards, collection centers etc.) to agri-cooperatives, farmer groups and/or private enterprises with matching grants from local municipalities and/or province and district level agencies like MoLMAC, ADD and AKC. Priority will be given to functional agri-cooperatives and/or agri-business private enterprises led by women, smallholders or those situated in remote locations. In consultation with the local municipalities, a detailed criteria and process for selection of the agri-cooperatives and enterprises will be developed and implemented. Care will be taken to ensure that agri-cooperatives and enterprises, which are badly hit by the pandemic and are likely to support vulnerable households, are selected. Support will also be extended to youth and returnee migrants on income generation opportunities along the various stages of value chain development for the identified commodities. Key activities will include technical training on select income generation activities such as small enterprises activities, mobile repair, plumbing, grocery start-up etc and provision of a start-up grant to encourage the trained participants to start their businesses. Those trainings will be provided to migrant returnees and vulnerable HHs by keeping 20 to 25 participants in each group based on their choice of training and these trainings will be conducted by technical staffs/consultants hired by partners and contents of those trainings will be based on the need and pre-test result of participants. Convergence with local municipalities will be explored to align with existing youth entrepreneurship development programs like the one being implemented by Tansen municipality. The trained project staff will provide necessary mentoring and troubleshooting support to the entrepreneurs in the beginning phase of their businesses. Markets play a strong role directly and indirectly in the livelihoods of smallholder farmers. The project will identify agribusinesses and SMEs which are critical to the livelihoods of the targeted communities and support them with cash grants and other technical support to help them recover from the impacts of COVID-19 and facilitate market recovery in the process. Other technical support could also include linking businesses with financial institutions for larger credit support, or with suppliers for favorable transaction terms. Where needed, the project will facilitate inclusion of women and other excluded groups into existing agriculture cooperatives and farmer groups to engage in commercial farming activities and increase their income to mitigate recent losses. Inclusion in these groups will also increase their access to various livelihood related government programs and subsidies. The project will provide trainings to targeted households on production and productivity enhancement for the crops/commodities identified through short-duration trainings and on-farm demonstrations where possible. Special attention will be paid to tailor the trainings (timing, duration, location, content, language, etc.) to facilitate meaningful participation from women. Following the trainings, the farmers will be provided with improved production inputs (seeds, planting material, seed treatment material, processing inputs, etc.) to encourage in adoption. These inputs will also help farmers restart their agricultural activities which might have been affected due to a shortage in availability of quality seeds and other inputs due to COVID-19 impact. The project will procure these inputs from local markets and vendors, providing support for the local economy and contributing to market recovery.

en.full: COVID-19 Emergency Response and Recovery Project (CERRP)
